article_title: Cognition Eyes a $25 Billion Valuation, Doubling in Seven Months as the AI Coding Agent Race Reprices

content_preview.title: Cognition Eyes a $25 Billion Valuation, Doubling in Seven Months as the AI Coding Agent Race Reprices
content_preview.summary: Devin's maker is in talks to raise hundreds of millions at $25 billion just seven months after a $10.2 billion round, putting fresh pressure on a sector already reshaped by the SpaceX-Cursor option and Anthropic-OpenAI competition.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Cognition AI, the San Francisco startup behind the autonomous software engineering agent Devin, is in early discussions to raise hundreds of millions of dollars at a roughly $25 billion valuation, [Bloomberg reported](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-04-23/ai-coding-firm-cognition-in-funding-talks-at-25-billion-value) on April 23. The proposed terms would more than double the $10.2 billion valuation Cognition reached in September 2025 — a pace that mirrors a broader repr...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt
1 source(s) blocked bot access — sources likely valid but not archivable automatically

editor_notes.content_quality: Round-2 rewrite addresses all four round-1 misattributions cleanly. Article is tighter at 622 words (was 643), still in News range. Tone is unchanged — neutral, hedged on early-stage talks, no editorializing. The new CNBC paragraph for the ARR-doubled claim reads more rigorously than the prior swapped attribution.
editor_notes.source_verification: Read all 6 local snapshots from sources/2026-04/cognition-eyes-a-25-billion-valuation-doubling-in-seven-months-as-the-ai-coding-agent-race-reprices/. Bloomberg snapshot still failed with HTTP 403 (same as round 1 — Bloomberg's anti-bot). Other 5 snapshots all 200 OK. Re-verified the two specifically corrected claims: (a) Microsoft as Devin enterprise customer is verbatim in Techzine source-2 ('Customers include Microsoft, Dell Technologies, and Cisco Systems'); (b) CNBC source-5 (Sept 8 2025) confirms 'Since acquiring Windsurf, Cognition's annual recurring revenue, or ARR, has more than doubled, Wu said.' SiliconANGLE/TechCrunch/CNBC are all on the allowlist. TechFundingNews dropped from sources cleanly (no claim was reattributed to it).
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All four round-1 attribution errors resolved: 'declined to comment' removed, Microsoft now correctly attributed to Techzine, ARR-doubled attributed directly to CNBC (with Scott Wu as the original speaker), 'Series C' label dropped. Bloomberg primary remains unverifiable (403), but the unverifiable direct quote was removed and only Bloomberg-attributed facts that SiliconANGLE corroborates remain. Minor wording nuance: the article says 'CEO Scott Wu told CNBC' while CNBC writes 'Wu said' citing his Monday blog post — the substance is correct, only the framing 'told CNBC' is slightly tighter than warranted (Wu addressed the public via blog post and CNBC quoted him). Not an editorial blocker.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Unchanged — neutral, professional, appropriately hedged. The reframed Context section avoids the unverifiable Bloomberg quote without losing the analytical thread.
editor_notes.originality: Same as round 1 — new topic, valid SpaceX-Cursor cross-references.
concerns: Bloomberg primary remains unverifiable (snapshot 403, live WebFetch 403). The remaining Bloomberg-attributed facts ($25B talks, hundreds of millions, April 23, terms in flux) are all corroborated by SiliconANGLE which is verified.
Techzine is the only source naming Microsoft and is not on the allowlist — reasonable trade outlet but flagged.
Minor wording: 'CEO Scott Wu told CNBC' could be tightened to 'as Wu said and CNBC reported' since Wu's primary venue was a blog post that CNBC quoted. Not a blocker.
recommendations: Optional: add techzine.eu to config/source_allowlist.txt to silence the warning.
Optional micro-edit: 'Wu said' rather than 'told CNBC' would more precisely match how CNBC frames the quote. Not blocking.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Round-2 rewrite cleanly resolves all four attribution errors. The Bloomberg unverifiability is the only residual concern and is structural (their anti-bot policy), already noted in round 1, and the unverifiable direct quote was correctly removed. Clean APPROVE — ready to merge.
round_1_findings_resolved: 'Cognition declined to comment' (SiliconANGLE attribution) — removed entirely.
Microsoft as customer — re-attributed to Techzine (verbatim match in snapshot).
ARR more than doubled (Techzine/CNBC swap) — attributed directly to CNBC source-5 (Wu's statement, Sept 8 2025).
$400M 'Series C' — label dropped; now reads '$400 million round led by Founders Fund'.
Bloomberg unverifiable direct quote — removed and reframed as bot's own analytical paraphrase.
